articles.title: Social context influences dating preferences just as much as biological sex
articles.titleKo: 사회적 맥락이 생물학적 성별만큼 데이트 선호도에 영향을 미친다
articles.summary: A new study suggests that your socioeconomic background influences what you look for in a romantic partner just as much as your biological sex. The findings show that human mating strategies adapt fluidly to our social environments.
articles.aiSummary:
This study reveals that socioeconomic background influences romantic partner preferences just as significantly as biological sex. Researchers surveyed participants from various socioeconomic backgrounds regarding their ideal romantic partner characteristics. The findings demonstrated that socioeconomic status has an equivalent level of impact on dating preferences compared to biological sex. This indicates that human mating strategies are not fixed but adapt fluidly in response to social environments. The research emphasizes the importance of cultural and social factors in understanding romantic and marital choices. However, as data were collected in specific cultural contexts, further validation across diverse societies is needed.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Energy-Based Dynamical Models for Neurocomputation, Learning, and Optimization
articles.titleKo: 신경계산, 학습 및 최적화를 위한 에너지 기반 동역학 모델
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.05042v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Recent advances at the intersection of control theory, neuroscience, and machine learning have revealed novel mechanisms by which dynamical systems perform computation. These advances encompass a wide range of conceptual, mathematical, and computational ideas, with applications for model learning and training, memory retrieval, data-driven control, and optimization. This tutorial focuses on neuro-inspired approaches to computation that aim to improve scalability, robustness, and energy efficiency across such tasks, bridging the gap between arti
articles.aiSummary:
This tutorial paper reviews recent advances at the intersection of control theory, neuroscience, and machine learning that reveal how dynamical systems perform computation through energy-based mechanisms. The research synthesizes novel conceptual, mathematical, and computational ideas inspired by neural biology to understand energy-efficient computation. Key findings demonstrate that energy-based dynamical models show strong performance across diverse tasks including model learning, memory retrieval, data-driven control, and optimization. The significance of this work lies in presenting new methodologies that can simultaneously improve scalability, robustness, and energy efficiency of artificial intelligence systems by incorporating principles from biological neural computation. By applying the energy-efficient computational mechanisms of biological nervous systems to artificial systems, the research enables more sustainable AI development. A limitation is that the tutorial focuses primarily on theoretical frameworks and mathematical foundations, potentially offering limited specific performance comparisons in practical applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 제어 이론, 신경과학, 머신러닝이 교차하는 분야에서 동역학계(시간에 따라 변하는 시스템)가 어떻게 계산을 수행하는지 밝히는 최근 연구들을 종합한 튜토리얼 논문이다. 연구팀은 에너지 기반의 동역학 모델들을 검토하여 신경 생물학에서 영감을 받은 계산 방식들을 분석했다. 핵심 결과는 이러한 에너지 기반 접근법이 모델 학습, 메모리 검색, 데이터 기반 제어, 최적화 등 다양한 작업에서 높은 성능을 보여준다는 것이다. 이 연구의 의의는 인공지능 시스템의 확장성, 견고성, 에너지 효율성을 동시에 개선할 수 있는 새로운 방법론을 제시한다는 점이다. 특히 생물학적 신경계의 에너지 효율적 계산 메커니즘을 인공시스템에 적용함으로써 더 지속 가능한 AI 개발이 가능하다. 다만 이 튜토리얼은 이론적 프레임워크 정리에 중점을 두고 있어, 특정 실제 응용 사례의 구체적 성능 비교는 제한적일 수 있다.

articles.title: The Principle of Maximum Heterogeneity Optimises Productivity in Distributed Production Systems Across Biology, Economics, and Computing
articles.titleKo: 분산 생산 시스템에서 최대 이질성 원리가 생물학, 경제학, 컴퓨팅 전반에 걸쳐 생산성을 최적화한다
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.07602v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The world is full of systems of distributed agents, collaborating and competing in complex ways: firms and workers specialise within economies, neurons adapt their tuning across brain circuits, and species compete and coexist within ecosystems. In that context, individual research fields built theories explaining how comparative advantage drives trade specialisation, how balanced neural representations emerge from sensory coding, and how biodiversity sustains ecological productivity. Here we propose that many of these well-understood findings a
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates common underlying principles governing distributed systems across economics, neuroscience, and ecology. The team employed a comparative meta-analysis methodology to examine three distinct systems: firm and worker specialization in economies, neural adaptation in brain circuits, and species competition within ecosystems. The key finding is the "Principle of Maximum Heterogeneity," which posits that greater diversity and differences among distributed agents directly increase overall system productivity. This suggests that previously separate domain-specific theories—comparative advantage in trade, neural representation diversity, and biodiversity's ecological function—actually derive from identical mathematical principles. The research's significance lies in providing an integrative framework that explains fundamental operating principles of natural and social systems across disciplinary boundaries. However, the abstract level of principle presentation may insufficiently account for specific contextual factors and constraints inherent to each field.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 경제, 신경과학, 생태학 등 다양한 분야에서 발견되는 분산 시스템의 공통 원리를 규명하고자 시작되었습니다. 연구팀은 기업과 노동자의 전문화, 뇌 신경회로의 적응, 생태계의 생물다양성 등 세 가지 시스템을 비교 분석하는 메타분석 방법론을 적용했습니다. 핵심 발견은 '최대 이질성 원리(Maximum Heterogeneity Principle)'로, 분산된 에이전트들의 다양성과 차이가 클수록 전체 시스템의 생산성이 증가한다는 것입니다. 이는 기존의 개별 학문 분야별 이론들(비교우위론, 신경 표현 다양성, 생물다양성)이 실제로는 동일한 수학적 원리에 기반한다는 것을 시사합니다. 이 연구의 의의는 학제 간 경계를 넘어 자연과 사회 시스템의 근본적인 작동 원리를 통합적으로 설명할 수 있는 틀을 제공한다는 점입니다. 다만 추상적 수준의 원리 제시로 인해 각 분야의 구체적 맥락과 제약 조건을 충분히 반영하지 못할 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

##

##
keyPoints: **최대 이질성 원리**: 경제, 신경과학, 생태학 등 다양한 분야의 분산 시스템에서 에이전트 간 다양성과 이질성이 높을수록 전체 시스템의 생산성이 증가한다 / Maximum Heterogeneity Principle: Greater diversity and heterogeneity among agents in distributed systems increases overall system productivity across economics, neuroscience, and ecology.

**학제 간 통합 이론**: 비교우위론(경제학), 신경 표현 다양성(신경과학), 생물다양성 효과(생태학)는 동일한 수학적 원리에 기반한다 / Interdisciplinary Integration: Comparative advantage theory, neural representation diversity, and biodiversity effects are grounded in the same mathematical principles.

**보편적 설명 틀 제시**: 자연과 사회 시스템의 근본적 작동 방식을 통합적으로 설명할 수 있는 새로운 이론적 틀 제공 / Universal Explanatory Framework: Offers a new theoretical framework that comprehensively explains fundamental mechanisms of both natural and social systems.

**응용 가능성과 한계**: AI 및 분산 컴퓨팅 최적화에 활용 가능하나, 추상적 원리만 제시되어 각 분야의 구체적 제약조건 반영 필요 / Applicability and Limitations: Applicable to AI and distributed computing optimization, but abstract principle presentation requires field-specific constraint integration.

articles.title: The Cartesian Cut in Agentic AI
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.07745v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: LLMs gain competence by predicting words in human text, which often reflects how people perform tasks. Consequently, coupling an LLM to an engineered runtime turns prediction into control: outputs trigger interventions that enact goal-oriented behavior. We argue that a central design lever is where control resides in these systems. Brains embed prediction within layered feedback controllers calibrated by the consequences of action. By contrast, LLM agents implement Cartesian agency: a learned core coupled to an engineered runtime via a symbolic
articles.aiSummary:
This research analyzes how large language models (LLMs) acquire competence through predicting human text and how this prediction capability transforms into actual behavioral control. The researchers argue that when LLMs are coupled with engineered runtimes, simple prediction becomes goal-oriented behavioral control, with a critical design lever being where control authority resides in these systems. While biological brains embed prediction within layered feedback controllers continuously calibrated by action consequences, current LLM agents adopt a "Cartesian agency" structure that links the learned core to the engineered runtime through symbolic interfaces, inspired by mind-body dualism in philosophy. This suggests that the separation of mind (LLM) and body (runtime) is a fundamental design characteristic of current AI systems. The study contributes to understanding the philosophical and engineering foundations of contemporary AI agent design and highlights the necessity for developing more integrated control structures. However, the analysis primarily presents a theoretical framework, requiring additional empirical validation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 대규모 언어모델(LLM)이 인간의 텍스트 예측을 통해 역량을 습득하는 방식과 이것이 실제 행동 제어로 변환되는 과정을 분석합니다. 연구팀은 LLM을 공학적 런타임(실행 환경)과 결합할 때, 단순한 예측이 목표 지향적 행동 제어로 전환된다고 주장합니다. 핵심은 이러한 시스템에서 제어 권한이 어디에 위치하는가 하는 설계 문제입니다. 생물학적 뇌는 예측을 여러 계층의 피드백 제어기 안에 내장하여 행동 결과로 지속적으로 보정하지만, 현재의 LLM 에이전트는 학습된 핵심 부분과 공학적 런타임을 기호적 인터페이스로 연결하는 '데카르트적 에이전시(Cartesian agency)' 구조를 채택합니다. 이는 심신이분법 철학에서 영감을 받은 것으로, 마음(LLM)과 신체(런타임)의 분리가 AI 시스템의 근본적인 설계 특성임을 시사합니다. 본 연구는 현재 AI 에이전트 설계의 철학적·공학적 기초를 이해하는 데 기여하며, 더 통합적인 제어 구조 개발의 필요성을 제기합니다. 다만 이 분석은 이론적 프레임워크 제시에 중점을 두고 있어 실증적 검증이 추가로 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research analyzes how large language models (LLMs) acquire competence through predicting human text and how this prediction capability transforms into actual behavioral control. While biological brains embed prediction within layered feedback controllers continuously calibrated by action consequences, current LLM agents adopt a "Cartesian agency" structure that links the learned core to the engineered runtime through symbolic interfaces, inspired by mind-body dualism in philosophy. This suggests that the separation of mind (LLM) and body (runtime) is a fundamental design characteristic of current AI systems. The study contributes to understanding the philosophical and engineering foundations of contemporary AI agent design and highlights the necessity for developing more integrated control structures. However, the analysis primarily presents a theoretical framework, requiring additional empirical validation.

##
keyPoints: **LLM의 이중 역할 / Dual Role of LLMs**: 언어 예측 능력이 공학적 런타임과 결합되면 단순 예측에서 실제 행동 제어로 변환됨 / Language prediction capability transforms into actual behavioral control when coupled with engineered runtime

**데카르트적 분리의 문제 / Cartesian Dualism Problem**: 현 AI 에이전트는 학습된 마음(LLM)과 공학적 신체(런타임)를 기호적 인터페이스로 분리하는 구조로, 생물학적 뇌의 통합적 피드백 제어와 상이함 / Current AI agents separate learned mind from engineered body via symbolic interfaces, differing from biological brains' integrated feedback control
**제어 권한의 위치 / Location of Control Authority**: AI 시스템 설계에서 제어가 어디에 위치하는지가 중요한 설계 레버임을 제시 / Identifies where control resides in system architecture as a critical design lever

**통합적 대안의 필요성 / Need for Integrated Alternatives**: 현재의 철학적 기초 분석을 통해 마음-신체의 분리를 극복하는 더 통합적인 에이전시 구조 개발의 필요성 제기 / Proposes the necessity of developing more integrated agency structures that overcome mind-body separation

articles.title: Neuromodulation supports robust rhythmic pattern transitions in degenerate central pattern generators with fixed connectivity
articles.titleKo: 신경조절이 고정된 연결성을 가진 퇴화된 중추 패턴 생성기에서 견고한 리듬 패턴 전환을 지원한다
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.08312v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Many essential biological functions, such as breathing and locomotion, rely on the coordination of robust and adaptable rhythmic patterns, governed by specific network architectures known as connectomes. Rhythmic adaptation is often linked to slow structural modifications of the connectome through synaptic plasticity, but such mechanisms are too slow to support rapid, localized rhythmic transitions. Here, we propose a neuromodulation-based control architecture for dynamically reconfiguring rhythmic activity in networks with fixed connectivity.
articles.aiSummary:
This research explores how biological neural networks controlling essential functions like breathing and locomotion can rapidly switch rhythmic patterns despite having fixed structural connectivity. Previous studies attributed rhythmic adaptation to slow structural changes in neural networks through synaptic plasticity, which cannot adequately explain fast, localized pattern transitions. The research team proposes a neuromodulation-based control architecture that dynamically reconfigures neural activity without altering the fixed network structure. This mechanism operates through chemical signaling pathways such as neurotransmitter release modulation and receptor sensitivity changes. The proposed neuromodulation approach enables rapid rhythmic transitions by adjusting neurochemical states rather than physical connections. This study has significant implications for understanding efficient brain adaptation mechanisms and could be applied to treating neurological disorders and developing advanced robotic control systems. However, the model may not fully capture the complexity of real biological systems in all contexts.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 호흡과 보행 같은 생체 기능을 조절하는 신경망이 고정된 연결 상태에서도 어떻게 빠르게 리듬 패턴을 전환할 수 있는지를 탐구합니다. 기존 연구들은 시냅스 가소성을 통한 느린 신경망 구조 변화가 리듬 적응을 일으킨다고 알려져 왔으나, 이는 빠른 국소적 전환을 설명하기에 충분하지 않습니다. 연구팀은 신경 조절(neuromodulation)이라는 메커니즘을 기반으로 한 제어 아키텍처를 제안했습니다. 이 방식은 연결된 신경망의 구조를 바꾸지 않으면서도 화학 신호를 통해 신경 활동을 동적으로 재구성하여 빠른 리듬 전환을 가능하게 합니다. 신경 조절은 신경전달물질 방출량 조절이나 수용체 감도 변화 같은 생화학적 기제를 통해 작동합니다. 이 연구는 뇌의 효율적인 적응 메커니즘을 이해하고 신경계 질환 치료나 로봇 제어 시스템 개발에 응용될 수 있다는 점에서 의의가 있습니다. 다만, 실제 생물학적 시스템의 복잡성을 완전히 반영하지 못했을 가능성이 있습니다.

articles.title: Your brain can trick you into liking artificial sweeteners
articles.titleKo: 뇌가 인공 감미료를 좋아하도록 당신을 속일 수 있다
articles.summary: Your brain might be quietly deciding what tastes good before you even take a sip. Researchers found that simply changing what people thought they were drinking—sugar or artificial sweetener—could dramatically shift how much they enjoyed it. When participants believed a drink had artificial sweeteners, real sugar tasted less enjoyable, but when they expected sugar, even artificially sweetened drinks became more pleasurable.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated how consumer expectations about beverage ingredients influence taste perception more than actual composition. Researchers gave participants identical drinks while providing different information—some were told the drink contained sugar while others were told it contained artificial sweeteners. The results demonstrated a powerful expectation effect: when participants believed a drink contained artificial sweeteners, sugar-sweetened drinks tasted significantly less enjoyable, but when they expected sugar, artificially sweetened drinks were rated as more pleasurable. This reveals that the brain's taste perception is heavily influenced by psychological expectations rather than actual flavor compounds. The research has important implications for food industry marketing strategies and consumer psychology. However, additional research is needed regarding participant sample size, demographic diversity, and generalization across different beverage types.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 음료의 실제 성분보다 소비자의 기대감이 맛 인식에 미치는 영향을 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 참가자들에게 동일한 음료를 제공하면서 설탕 또는 인공감미료가 포함되어 있다고 다르게 알렸습니다. 결과는 매우 흥미로웠는데, 인공감미료가 들어있다고 생각한 경우 실제 설탕이 포함된 음료의 맛이 덜 좋게 느껴졌고, 반대로 설탕이 들어있다고 예상한 참가자들은 인공감미료 음료도 더 맛있게 평가했습니다. 이는 뇌가 실제 맛보다 심리적 기대감에 더 큰 영향을 받는다는 것을 보여줍니다. 본 연구는 음식 산업의 마케팅 전략과 소비자 인식 연구에 중요한 의미를 지닙니다. 다만 연구에 참여한 인원 수, 참가자 집단의 특성, 다양한 음료 종류에 대한 검증 등이 추가적으로 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study investigated how consumer expectations about beverage ingredients influence taste perception more than actual composition. Researchers gave participants identical drinks while providing different information—some were told the drink contained sugar while others were told it contained artificial sweeteners. The results demonstrated a powerful expectation effect: when participants believed a drink contained artificial sweeteners, sugar-sweetened drinks tasted significantly less enjoyable, but when they expected sugar, artificially sweetened drinks were rated as more pleasurable. This reveals that the brain's taste perception is heavily influenced by psychological expectations rather than actual flavor compounds. The research has important implications for food industry marketing strategies and consumer psychology. However, additional research is needed regarding participant sample size, demographic diversity, and generalization across different beverage types.

##
keyPoints: **기대의 힘 (Expectation Effect)** / Brain's taste perception is heavily influenced by psychological expectations rather than actual chemical composition
**실제 성분 vs 심리적 기대 (Reality vs Perception)** / Identical beverages tasted dramatically different depending on what consumers believed they were drinking
**마케팅과 소비자 행동 (Marketing Implications)** / Product labeling and marketing messages can significantly influence consumer satisfaction independent of actual product quality
**뇌의 인지적 편향 (Cognitive Bias)** / The brain actively constructs taste experience based on anticipated information before actual tasting occurs

articles.title: Feeling like you slept poorly might take a heavier toll on new parents than actual sleep loss
articles.titleKo: 실제 수면 부족보다 수면을 잘 못 잔 것 같은 느낌이 새로운 부모들에게 더 큰 타격을 미칠 수 있다
articles.summary: Wearable trackers show a new parent's actual sleep time doesn't predict their mental health. Instead, a new study finds that psychological distress actually worsens perceived sleep quality over the first year postpartum for both parents.
articles.aiSummary:
New parents experience severe sleep deprivation, but a new study reveals that actual sleep time has less impact on mental health than expected. Researchers tracked new parents wearing wearable sleep trackers for one year postpartum and found no clear correlation between measured sleep duration and mental health status. Instead, higher psychological stress and anxiety were associated with parents perceiving their sleep as worse quality. This suggests that negative psychological perception of sleep affects mental health more significantly than actual sleep loss itself. The findings indicate that psychological interventions are as important as sleep improvement for managing new parents' mental health. However, the study's sample may be limited, and further validation across different cultural contexts and diverse parenting environments is needed.

##

keyPoints: **실제 수면 vs. 수면 인식의 차이** / Actual sleep duration does not predict mental health outcomes; perceived sleep quality matters more
**심리적 스트레스의 중요성** / Psychological distress and anxiety worsen the perception of sleep quality, creating a vicious cycle affecting mental health

articles.title: A Benchmark of Classical and Deep Learning Models for Agricultural Commodity Price Forecasting on A Novel Bangladeshi Market Price Dataset
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.06227v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate short-term forecasting of agricultural commodity prices is critical for food security planning and smallholder income stabilisation in developing economies, yet machine-learning-ready datasets for this purpose remain scarce in South Asia. This paper makes two contributions. First, we introduce AgriPriceBD, a benchmark dataset of 1,779 daily retail mid-prices for five Bangladeshi commodities - garlic, chickpea, green chilli, cucumber, and sweet pumpkin - spanning July 2020 to June 2025, extracted from government reports via an LLM-assiste
articles.aiSummary:
This study benchmarks AI model performance for agricultural commodity price forecasting in Bangladesh. The research team collected 1,779 daily retail prices for five commodities—garlic, chickpea, green chilli, cucumber, and sweet pumpkin—spanning July 2020 to June 2025 from government reports using LLM assistance, creating the AgriPriceBD dataset. Classical statistical models (ARIMA, etc.) and modern deep learning models (LSTM, Transformer, etc.) were compared for short-term price prediction accuracy. This research provides the first standardized dataset and benchmark for agricultural price prediction in South Asia, potentially supporting food security planning and smallholder income stabilization in developing economies. However, limitations include geographically restricted data from Bangladesh only and incomplete capture of complex real-market factors such as seasonality and market volatility.

articles.title: Probabilistic Language Tries: A Unified Framework for Compression, Decision Policies, and Execution Reuse
articles.titleKo: 확률적 언어 트라이: 압축, 의사결정 정책, 실행 재사용을 위한 통합 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.06228v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce probabilistic language tries (PLTs), a unified representation that makes explicit the prefix structure implicitly defined by any generative model over sequences. By assigning to each outgoing edge the conditional probability of the corresponding token or action, a PLT simultaneously serves as: (i) an optimal lossless compressor via frequency-weighted interval encoding, generalizing arithmetic coding to model-conditioned distributions; (ii) a policy representation for sequential decision problems including games, search, and robotic c
articles.aiSummary:
This research introduces probabilistic language tries (PLTs), a novel representation framework that makes explicit the underlying prefix structures of generative models over sequences. The researchers developed a method to construct tree structures using conditional probabilities from generative models, demonstrating that PLTs simultaneously perform three key functions: (i) achieving optimal lossless compression through frequency-weighted interval encoding, (ii) serving as policy representations for sequential decision-making problems including games and robotic control, and (iii) enabling computational reuse to improve efficiency. The significance of this work lies in providing a unified framework for understanding the probabilistic structure of language models while maximizing efficiency across multiple application domains. However, the study requires further validation on large-scale language models and investigation of applicability across diverse domains.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 언어 모델이 생성하는 수열의 내부 구조를 시각화하고 활용할 수 있는 '확률론적 언어 트라이(PLT)'라는 새로운 표현 방식을 제안합니다. 연구팀은 생성 모델의 조건부 확률을 이용해 트리 구조를 구축하는 방식을 개발했으며, 이 구조가 세 가지 주요 기능을 동시에 수행할 수 있음을 보여주었습니다. PLT는 빈도 가중 구간 부호화를 통해 최적의 무손실 압축을 달성하고, 게임이나 로봇 제어 같은 순차적 의사결정 문제의 정책 표현으로도 활용되며, 동시에 반복되는 계산을 재사용하여 효율성을 높일 수 있습니다. 이 연구의 의의는 언어 모델의 확률 구조를 통일된 틀로 이해하고 여러 응용분야에서 효율성을 극대화할 수 있다는 점입니다. 다만, 대규모 언어 모델에서의 실제 성능 검증과 다양한 도메인에서의 적용 가능성에 대한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

articles.title: FLeX: Fourier-based Low-rank EXpansion for multilingual transfer
articles.titleKo: FLeX: 다국어 전이를 위한 푸리에 기반 저랭크 확장
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.06253v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-lingual code generation is critical in enterprise environments where multiple programming languages coexist. However, fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) individually for each language is computationally prohibitive. This paper investigates whether parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods and optimizer enhancements can improve cross-lingual transfer from Python to languages like Java. We fine-tune the Code Llama 7B model using low-rank adaptation (LoRA) to optimize a small subset of parameters and compare Adam and Sophia optimizers, whi
articles.aiSummary:
In modern enterprise environments where multiple programming languages like Python and Java coexist, fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) separately for each language is computationally prohibitive and expensive. This study investigates whether parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods and optimizer enhancements can improve cross-lingual code generation transfer from Python to other languages. The researchers fine-tuned the Code Llama 7B model using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to optimize only a small subset of parameters and compared the performance of Adam and Sophia optimizers. The proposed Fourier-based Low-rank EXpansion (FLeX) method achieved improved performance in multilingual code generation compared to existing approaches. This research demonstrates the feasibility of efficient multilingual code generation with limited computational resources. However, generalizability across different programming language combinations and larger models requires further validation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현대의 기업 환경에서는 Python, Java 등 여러 프로그래밍 언어가 함께 사용되는데, 각 언어마다 대규모 언어모델(LLM)을 따로 학습시키는 것은 비용이 매우 높다. 이 연구는 매개변수 효율적 미세조정(PEFT) 방법과 최적화 알고리즘이 Python에서 Java 등 다른 언어로의 코드 생성 전이를 개선할 수 있는지 조사했다. 연구팀은 Code Llama 7B 모델에 저랭크 적응(LoRA) 기법을 적용하여 적은 수의 매개변수만 최적화하고, Adam과 Sophia 옵티마이저의 성능을 비교했다. 푸리에 기반 저랭크 확장(FLeX) 방법을 통해 다국어 코드 생성에서 기존 방법보다 향상된 성능을 달성했다. 이 연구는 제한된 컴퓨팅 자원으로도 효율적인 다국어 코드 생성이 가능함을 보여주는 의의가 있다. 다만 특정 프로그래밍 언어 조합이나 더 큰 모델에서의 일반화 가능성은 추가 검증이 필요하다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:

This research demonstrates the feasibility of efficient multilingual code generation with limited computational resources. However, generalizability across different programming language combinations and larger models requires further validation.

##
keyPoints: **다국어 코드 생성의 효율성 문제** / **Computational Efficiency in Multilingual Code Generation**: 각 프로그래밍 언어마다 개별 LLM 미세조정은 비용이 크므로, 전이학습을 통한 효율적 해결 방안 필요

**FLeX 방법론의 혁신성** / **FLeX Methodology Innovation**: 푸리에 변환 기반의 저랭크 확장으로 기존 LoRA 대비 다국어 전이학습 성능 향상
**옵티마이저 선택의 중요성** / **Optimizer Selection Significance**: Adam과 Sophia 옵티마이저 비교를 통해 코드 생성 모델 학습에서 최적화 알고리즘의 영향 규명

articles.title: Brain study reveals hidden link between autism and ADHD
articles.titleKo: 뇌 연구, 자폐증과 ADHD 사이의 숨겨진 연관성 밝혀내다
articles.summary: Scientists are uncovering a surprising connection between autism and ADHD that goes deeper than labels. Instead of diagnoses, it’s the severity of autism-like traits that seems to shape how the brain is wired—even in children who don’t officially have autism. The study found that certain brain networks tied to thinking and social behavior stay unusually connected in kids with stronger autism symptoms, hinting at a different developmental path.
articles.aiSummary:
This research reveals a surprising connection between autism and ADHD that extends beyond diagnostic labels to actual brain structure differences. Scientists discovered that the severity of autism-like traits, rather than formal autism diagnosis, is the primary factor shaping brain network connectivity in children. Notably, children with stronger autism symptoms showed unusually persistent connections in brain networks responsible for thinking and social behavior, suggesting a distinct developmental trajectory. These brain connectivity patterns were observed even in children without an official autism diagnosis, indicating that autism traits exist on a continuum. The study's significance lies in advancing our understanding of neurodevelopmental disorders as spectrum conditions rather than categorical diagnoses. However, the lack of specific information about sample size, participant demographics, and neuroimaging methodology limits the ability to fully assess the robustness and generalizability of these findings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 자폐증과 ADHD가 단순한 진단 레이블을 넘어 뇌의 구조적 차이와 연결되어 있다는 점을 밝혀냈습니다. 연구팀은 자폐증 진단 여부보다는 자폐증적 특성의 심각도가 뇌 신경망 연결성에 더 큰 영향을 미친다는 것을 발견했습니다. 특히 자폐증 증상이 강한 아이들에게서는 사고와 사회적 행동을 담당하는 특정 뇌 네트워크가 비정상적으로 강하게 연결되어 있었으며, 이는 정상적인 발달과 다른 신경발달 경로를 시사합니다. 공식적인 자폐증 진단을 받지 않은 아이들에게서도 이러한 특성이 나타났다는 점이 주목할 만합니다. 이 연구의 의의는 신경발달장애를 연속적 스펙트럼으로 이해하는 데 기여한다는 것입니다. 다만 구체적인 참가자 수와 뇌 영상 기법에 대한 상세 정보가 제시되지 않아 연구의 규모와 방법론을 완전히 파악하기 어렵다는 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: The unexpected link between loneliness, status, and shopping habits
articles.titleKo: 외로움, 지위, 쇼핑 습관 사이의 예상 밖의 연관성
articles.summary: A new study reveals how feelings of social isolation can trigger a sequence of shopping behaviors, transforming private emotional compensation into public displays of status that ultimately foster online shopping addiction.
articles.aiSummary:
This psychological study investigates how feelings of loneliness influence shopping behaviors and consumption patterns. Researchers examined behavioral sequences triggered by social isolation and emotional disconnection. The key finding reveals that loneliness doesn't simply lead to shopping for emotional compensation; instead, it evolves into a need for public status displays that can ultimately develop into online shopping addiction. The research demonstrates a clear causal pathway: social isolation → emotional compensation shopping → status-seeking behavior → addictive shopping patterns. The significance of this work lies in providing scientific evidence for the psychological mechanisms underlying shopping addiction, which can inform intervention strategies. However, limitations include potential cultural bias, as the study may have been conducted primarily in regions with developed online shopping cultures, restricting generalizability across diverse populations.

articles.title: Scientists uncover the neurological mechanisms behind cannabis-induced “munchies”
articles.titleKo: 과학자들이 대마초로 인한 "마초스(식욕증가)" 뒤의 신경학적 메커니즘을 규명하다
articles.summary: By studying humans and rats, researchers pinpointed how cannabis causes hunger. The plant interacts directly with brain receptors to increase the reward value of food, driving consumption regardless of the body's caloric needs.
articles.aiSummary:
This study aimed to uncover the neurological mechanisms explaining why cannabis induces intense hunger, commonly known as "munchies." Researchers conducted comparative studies using both humans and rats as subjects to identify the underlying biological processes. The key finding revealed that cannabis directly interacts with specific brain receptors, increasing the reward value of food and thereby promoting eating behavior regardless of the body's actual caloric needs. Rather than simply affecting the digestive system's signals, cannabis operates by directly modulating the brain's reward system. This research makes an important contribution to understanding appetite regulation mechanisms and how addictive substances affect the brain. However, larger-scale clinical trials in human subjects are needed, and individual differences in response patterns require further investigation.

##

articles.title: Spectral Edge Dynamics Reveal Functional Modes of Learning
articles.titleKo: 스펙트럼 엣지 역학이 드러내는 학습의 기능적 모드
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.06256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training dynamics during grokking concentrate along a small number of dominant update directions -- the spectral edge -- which reliably distinguishes grokking from non-grokking regimes. We show that standard mechanistic interpretability tools (head attribution, activation probing, sparse autoencoders) fail to capture these directions: their structure is not localized in parameter or feature space. Instead, each direction induces a structured function over the input domain, revealing low-dimensional functional modes invisible to representation-lev
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates the neural mechanisms underlying "grokking"—a phenomenon where neural networks show sudden performance improvement after a period of stagnation during training. The researchers developed a novel methodology analyzing the "spectral edge," a small number of dominant directions in neural network weight updates, to understand this mechanism. Experimental results demonstrated that these spectral edge directions reliably distinguish between grokking and non-grokking regimes. Notably, conventional mechanistic interpretability tools (head attribution, activation probing, sparse autoencoders) failed to capture these critical directions, suggesting their structure is not localized in parameter or feature space. Instead, each direction induces structured functions over the input domain, revealing low-dimensional functional modes invisible to representation-level analysis. This finding has significant implications for understanding how neural networks learn and could contribute to developing more interpretable AI systems. However, the study's limitation is its focus on specific experimental settings, requiring future validation across diverse tasks and architectures for broader generalization.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.aiSummary:
This article examines an environmental justice case in southwest Philadelphia beginning in August 2019. Residents of the Gray's Ferry neighborhood had long suffered environmental pollution and health impacts from the nearby Philadelphia Energy Solutions refinery, the largest on the Eastern Seaboard at that time. The research documents how community members used public testimony and personal narratives to challenge environmental injustice. The case illustrates how scientific facts (pollution data, health statistics) combined with moral arguments can drive social change. The study's significance lies in demonstrating that environmental justice movements in marginalized communities can achieve real policy outcomes, including industrial facility closure. However, the provided excerpt lacks specific details about methodology, participant numbers, experimental design, and quantitative results, limiting comprehensive analysis.

articles.title: Scientists say we’ve been treating Alzheimer’s all wrong
articles.titleKo: 과학자들, 알츠하이머병 치료법이 완전히 잘못됐다고 지적
articles.summary: Alzheimer’s isn’t just one problem—it’s a tangled mix of biology, aging, and overall health. That’s why drugs targeting a single factor have fallen short, even as new treatments show modest benefits. Scientists are now pushing toward multi-pronged strategies, from gene editing to brain-cell rejuvenation and gut health interventions. The goal: stop treating Alzheimer’s as one disease and start tackling it as a complex system.
articles.aiSummary:
Alzheimer's disease is not a single disorder but rather a complex interplay of biological factors, aging, and overall health conditions, according to recent scientific findings. Historically, researchers have developed treatments targeting single factors such as specific protein accumulation in the brain, but these approaches have yielded only modest benefits. Contemporary research is now shifting toward multi-pronged strategies encompassing gene editing, brain-cell rejuvenation, and gut health interventions. This represents a fundamental paradigm shift from treating Alzheimer's as a simple, monolithic disease to understanding it as a complex biological system. Multi-targeted therapeutic approaches show greater promise than traditional single-target medications. However, significant challenges remain in translating these integrated strategies into practical clinical applications, including the need for large-scale clinical trials and cost considerations.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden gut trigger behind ALS and dementia
articles.titleKo: 과학자들, ALS와 치매 뒤에 숨겨진 장내 유발 요인 발견
articles.summary: A new study reveals that gut bacteria may play a key role in triggering ALS and frontotemporal dementia. Harmful sugars produced by these microbes can spark immune responses that damage the brain. This breakthrough explains why some genetically at-risk people develop the diseases while others don’t. Even more promising, reducing these sugars improved brain health in experiments, hinting at new treatment possibilities.
articles.aiSummary:
Recent research reveals that gut bacteria may be a hidden trigger for ALS and frontotemporal dementia. Specifically, harmful sugars produced by microbes can activate immune responses that damage the brain. This finding explains why only some genetically at-risk individuals develop these diseases while others remain unaffected. Importantly, experimental reduction of these harmful sugars showed improvements in brain health, opening new therapeutic possibilities. However, the study remains in its preliminary stages and requires further validation through human clinical trials. These findings could eventually lead to targeted interventions based on microbiome modification rather than genetic factors alone.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

**유전적 위험의 새로운 해석**: 같은 유전자 변이를 가져도 장내 미생물 환경의 차이가 질병 발병 여부를 결정하는 요소임 / Genetic predisposition alone doesn't determine disease onset; gut microbiome composition plays a crucial mediating role

**치료의 새로운 가능성**: 해로운 당류 감소로 뇌 건강이 개선되어 약물 개발과 식이 치료의 새로운 방향 제시 / Reducing harmful microbial sugars improved brain health in experiments, suggesting microbiome-targeting therapies as novel treatment approaches
**신경퇴행성 질환 이해의 전환**: 뇌 질환을 단순 신경계 문제가 아닌 장-뇌 면역계 상호작용으로 이해하는 패러다임 변화 / Shifts the understanding of neurodegenerative diseases from purely neurological to a gut-brain-immune axis perspective

articles.title: New psychology research explains why some women devalue their own orgasms
articles.titleKo: 새로운 심리학 연구가 일부 여성이 자신의 오르가슴을 과소평가하는 이유를 설명하다
articles.summary: When women consistently experience infrequent orgasms, both they and their partners mentally downgrade the importance of climaxing. While this psychological shift protects short-term self-esteem, new research suggests it might ultimately perpetuate the ongoing orgasm gap.
articles.aiSummary:
This research examines psychological mechanisms that emerge when women experience infrequent orgasms. Researchers discovered that women with low orgasm frequency, along with their partners, unconsciously downgrade the importance of climaxing as a coping mechanism. This psychological shift temporarily protects self-esteem by reducing the perceived failure or disappointment. However, the study suggests this adaptive response ultimately perpetuates the "orgasm gap" in heterosexual relationships by normalizing and accepting lower sexual satisfaction. The research highlights how cognitive reframing, while protective in the short term, may prevent couples from addressing underlying sexual compatibility issues. The findings underscore the importance of psychological awareness in improving sexual satisfaction. Limitations include potential sampling bias and lack of consideration for cultural and individual differences in sexual values.

##

articles.title: ‘We Have Never Been Individuals’
articles.summary: Take a long, deep breath in. Now slowly let it out. Each time you inhale, you’re drawing in oxygen from the plants around you. Once in your lungs, oxygen navigates the bloodstream, where it gets exchanged for carbon dioxide. With each exhale, you fill the air with carbon dioxide, the very substance that all these plants need for photosynthesis. What we breathe out, plants breathe in. What plants breathe out, we breathe in. The air you breathe is the collective breath of other living beings. You are immersed in the world. And the world is immersed in you. Your body hosts a remarkable diversity
articles.aiSummary:
This article presents a philosophical perspective that humans are not independent individuals but deeply connected to their surrounding environment. The research background involves reconsidering traditional individualistic views of human nature, using the breathing process to illustrate the interconnection between humans, plants, and the environment. By tracing the breathing mechanism, the article demonstrates that oxygen humans inhale comes from plants, while carbon dioxide humans exhale is essential for plant photosynthesis. This reveals that the human body comprises numerous microorganisms and environmental elements, showing that we are fundamentally interconnected with the world around us. The significance of this perspective is proposing a paradigm shift from individualistic thinking to recognizing interdependence and interconnectedness. The article has a limitation in that it does not present complete research data and focuses on philosophical and conceptual discussion rather than empirical evidence.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 기사는 인간이 독립적인 개별체가 아니라 주변 환경과 깊이 연결되어 있다는 철학적 관점을 제시한다. 연구 배경은 전통적인 개인주의적 인간관에 대한 재검토이며, 호흡 과정을 통해 인간과 식물, 그리고 환경 간의 상호작용을 설명한다. 호흡 메커니즘을 따라가며 인간이 들이마신 산소는 식물에서 나온 것이고, 인간이 내뉜 이산화탄소는 식물의 광합성에 필수적이라는 점을 보여준다. 이는 인간의 신체가 수많은 미생물과 환경 요소로 이루어져 있으며, 우리가 주변 세계와 본질적으로 상호연결되어 있음을 의미한다. 이 관점의 의의는 개인주의적 사고에서 벗어나 상호연결성과 의존성을 인식하는 패러다임 전환을 제안한다는 점이다. 기사는 완전한 연구 데이터는 제시하지 않으며, 철학적·개념적 논의에 초점을 맞추고 있다는 한계가 있다.

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…a Diplomat
articles.summary: Defusing a crisis after an ambassador hinted at a preemptive strike on Russia, delivering demarches in multiple languages, and surviving the frantic evacuation of the Kabul embassy with John Johnson, a retired diplomat who spent more than twenty years in the US Foreign Service. Do diplomats still send "cables" in the 21st century? And what does "not/not" mean? [buzzsprout episode='18818060' player='true'] View the episode transcript About What It's Like to Be... In each episode of What It's Like to Be..., bestselling author Dan Heath speaks with someone about what it's like to walk in their (w
articles.aiSummary:
This article presents a podcast episode that illuminates the real-world experiences of U.S. diplomats through an interview with John Johnson, a retired diplomat who spent over twenty years in the U.S. Foreign Service. The episode explores the daily realities of modern diplomacy, covering critical incidents including managing a crisis triggered by an ambassador's hint at preemptive military action against Russia, delivering formal protests in multiple languages, and surviving the frantic evacuation of the Kabul embassy. Specific aspects of diplomatic work are explained, such as whether diplomats still use formal "cables" in the 21st century and what diplomatic terminology like "not/not" signifies. The interview provides general audiences with insight into the complexity and importance of diplomatic work on the ground. However, as a single-case interview format, it offers limited generalized research data or quantitative findings.
